name: AI Landing Zone
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Virtual Network
services.purpose: Hub-spoke topology with subnets for AI, data, and management tiers

dev.note: VNet itself is free, peering minimal

services.name: Private Endpoints
services.purpose: Private connectivity to Azure OpenAI, Key Vault, Storage, and AI Search

services.name: Azure Firewall
services.purpose: Centralized network security — egress filtering, threat intelligence, IDPS

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: Centralized secrets, certificates, and encryption keys for all AI services

services.name: Azure Policy
services.purpose: Governance — enforce tagging, allowed regions, SKU restrictions, RBAC compliance

services.name: Log Analytics
services.purpose: Centralized logging, diagnostics, and KQL alerting for all landing zone resources

services.name: Microsoft Defender for Cloud
services.purpose: Threat protection, vulnerability scanning, security posture management

services.name: Azure Bastion
services.purpose: Secure RDP/SSH access to VMs without public IPs for management operations

optimization_tips: Use Azure Firewall Basic SKU for dev/test — saves ~$650/month vs Standard
Share a single hub VNet across multiple spoke landing zones to amortize firewall cost
Use Azure Policy deny effects to prevent accidental expensive resource creation
Enable Defender for Cloud only on production subscriptions — dev can use free tier
Use VNet service endpoints instead of private endpoints where compliance allows — free alternative
Set Log Analytics daily caps in dev environments to avoid surprise ingestion costs
